specs.reason: R1 Complete AST Representation — the internal AST (Module/Stmt with 24 variants/Expr with 30+ variants) is the primary backing for issue #668 (ast module wrapper). Pre-clarification Q1-Q2 cite parser/ast.rs by name: Q1 confirms pub Module/Stmt/Expr exist in Rust but are not exposed to Python runtime; Q2 specifies the Python-facing wrapper must present CPython-compatible AST with matching field names and node types (e.g., ast.FunctionDef, ast.If, ast.BinOp) mapped from the internal representation. R2 PEP 695 Type Parameter Nodes (type_params on FnDef/ClassDef/EnumDef/TypeAlias) directly enable type[T] generic parameters. R3 TypeExpr enum covers annotation representations (Named/Generic/Optional/Union/Fn/Tuple). R4 Span Attachment via Spanned<T> is critical — every AST node carries byte offsets mapping directly to CPython's ast.Node lineno/col_offset fields. All four requirements (R1-R4) are MANDATORY for implementing ast module.

R1 Tokenization — defines the complete TokenKind enum (keywords, literals, operators, delimiters, synthetic) that maps 1:1 to Python's tokenize module constants. Pre-clarification Q2-Q3 establish the tokenize module (#669) requirement without explicit crate cites, but lexer/tokens-and-indent.md is the direct substrate: generate_tokens() must yield TokenInfo objects wrapping Mamba's Token struct (kind, start, end byte offsets from token.rs). R2 INDENT/DEDENT Generation via IndentProcessor state machine is CRITICAL because tokenize must expose INDENT/DEDENT tokens matching Python's tokenize.INDENT/DEDENT constants — this is the core use case. R3 PEP 701 f-string lexing enables FStr token output matching Python's FSTRING_START/FSTRING_END or STRING semantics. R4 String Literal Forms (Str/TripleStr/RawStr/ByteStr) all map to Python's STRING token type. All four requirements (R1-R4) are MANDATORY for implementing tokenize module.

R1 MIR Instruction Set defines all operations (LoadConst, LoadVar, StoreVar, GetAttr, SetAttr, GetItem, SetItem, BinOp, UnaryOp, BuildList, BuildDict, BuildSet, BuildTuple, Call, Raise, ExceptSetup) that directly map to dis.Instruction objects for issue #667 (dis module). Pre-clarification Q3 explicitly cites mir/mod.rs: 'Mamba uses MIR (mir/mod.rs with MirBody, BasicBlock, MirInst, Terminator) + Cranelift for codegen' and recommends 'exposing MIR-level instructions for debugging Mamba internals'. R1 instruction variants become dis.Instruction with opname/argval fields. R2 Basic Block Structure (Jump/Branch/Return terminators) maps to dis iteration unit — get_instructions() must iterate MirBlock sequences. R3 Function Representation (MirFunction with params/locals/blocks) maps to the dis.code object analogue. R4 Exception Handling Blocks (ExceptSetup/handler stack) enables dis to expose exception handler structures. R5 Extern Declarations (mb_* runtime symbol imports) provides runtime function references. All five requirements (R1-R5) are MANDATORY for implementing dis module.

All three new modules (ast, dis, tokenize, issues #668/#667/#669) must follow the native stdlib module registration pattern defined here. Pre-clarification Q1 states 'need new Python-facing wrapper layer' — this exactly matches the pattern in native-implementations.md. R2 Module Rewrite defines the file structure (new ast_mod.rs/dis_mod.rs/tokenize_mod.rs in runtime/stdlib) and the registration call-out points. R3 Refined Symbol Registration via register_symbols() macro-driven registry enables the three new modules to expose their symbols to the JIT module (e.g., module_registry.register('ast', {...}), then symbols.rs collects and registers all symbols). R4 Error Mapping layer converts Rust errors (parse errors from lexer/parser, MIR errors from lowering, etc.) into MbException with appropriate types and messages. R2-R4 define the integration pattern that ast/dis/tokenize implementations must follow. Medium relevance because it is architecturally prescriptive (all three must follow this pattern) but not implementable in isolation — implementation of each module (ast.md, dis.md, tokenize.md) takes precedence.

R3 inspect.isfunction/isclass/ismethod and R5 inspect.signature provide background context on Python introspection API patterns. R3 classification predicates (isfunction, isclass) relate to how ast.NodeVisitor might classify AST node types; R5 Signature/Parameter model aligns with how ast.FnDef parameter representation should map to Python's inspect.Parameter semantics. However, this spec operates at the runtime object inspection layer, not the compiler-internal exposure layer of ast/dis/tokenize modules. Value is reference context for API design alignment only — typing/inspect are peripheral to core module implementation but useful as precedent for parameter/attribute structure design. Listed at LOW to provide comprehensive architectural context without overstating direct implementation dependency.

notes: REVISION SUMMARY: Addressed all six reviewer issues from initial reference context FAIL verdict. (1) Added parser/ast.md (HIGH) — PRIMARY backing spec for issue #668 (ast module wrapper). Pre-clarifications Q1-Q2 explicitly cite parser/ast.rs; spec covers R1 (Complete AST Representation with 24 Stmt variants and 30+ Expr variants), R2 (PEP 695 type_params on generics), R3 (TypeExpr annotations), R4 (Span Attachment mapping to CPython lineno/col_offset). (2) Added lexer/tokens-and-indent.md (HIGH) — PRIMARY backing spec for issue #669 (tokenize module). Spec covers R1 (complete TokenKind enum matching Python tokenize constants), R2 (INDENT/DEDENT IndentProcessor state machine — critical for tokenize.INDENT/DEDENT tokens), R3 (PEP 701 f-string), R4 (String Literal Forms). (3) Added mir/mir.md (HIGH) — PRIMARY backing spec for issue #667 (dis module). Pre-clarification Q3 explicitly cites mir/mod.rs with MirBody/BasicBlock/MirInst/Terminator; spec covers R1 (MirInst instruction variants → dis.Instruction objects), R2 (BasicBlock/Jump/Branch/Return → dis iteration), R3 (MirFunction → dis.code analogue), R4 (exception handling), R5 (extern declarations). (4) Added stdlib/native-implementations.md (MEDIUM) — integration pattern spec. All three new modules must follow R2 (Module Rewrite in runtime/stdlib/*.rs), R3 (Refined Symbol Registration via register_symbols()), R4 (Error Mapping to MbException). Medium relevance because architecturally prescriptive but not implementable in isolation. (5) Added stdlib/typing-and-inspect.md (LOW) — background context only. R3/R5 provide precedent for classification predicates and Parameter/Signature model alignment with ast.FnDef; value is API design alignment, not direct implementation dependency. (6) BLOCKED: Cannot populate reference context with NEW specs (stdlib/ast.md, stdlib/dis.md, stdlib/tokenize.md) because they do not exist yet. These are creation deliverables for the change-spec phase, not existing specs. Reference context lists EXISTING specs only. FINAL COVERAGE: Issue #668 (ast module) covered by parser/ast.md + stdlib/native-implementations.md. Issue #667 (dis module) covered by mir/mir.md + stdlib/native-implementations.md. Issue #669 (tokenize module) covered by lexer/tokens-and-indent.md + stdlib/native-implementations.md. All pre-clarification questions Q1-Q3 now have spec coverage with explicit requirement mappings.
